questions.prompt: Minimum viable structure for a single-product DTC e-commerce brand?

choices.text: 4-6 campaigns: Brand Search (exact match, separate budget, captures branded queries before PMax), 1-2 Non-brand Search (high-intent keywords, broad + phrase with negatives), 1-2 PMax (cross-surface acquisition with asset groups by audience or product line), 1 Demand Gen (top-of-funnel video), and retargeting usually folded into PMax via audience signals. Fewer campaigns = each ad set reaches learning threshold; consolidated signal; less audience overlap.

questions.explanation: 4-6 campaigns: Brand + Non-brand Search + PMax + Demand Gen.

questions.prompt: Account has 30 ad groups each with 1 keyword and a dedicated campaign. Performance poor. What to do?

choices.text: Consolidate aggressively. Single-keyword ad groups are a 2015 anti-pattern that starves Smart Bidding of conversion volume — each ad group sits below the 30-conv/30-day threshold and learning never stabilizes. Merge into 3-8 ad groups per campaign organized by intent or product category, each with 5-15 broad-match keywords. Move to a 4-6 campaign account structure (Brand + Non-brand Search + PMax + Demand Gen). Concentrating conversion signal at the ad-group level lets the algorithm finally bid accurately.
More keywords per ad group only.

questions.explanation: Consolidate to 3-8 ad groups/campaign, 5-15 keywords each. Concentrate signal.
